stem: A sample of a certain isotope takes 35 years to decay to half its original mass. The function m(t) = 320(0.5)t/35 gives the approximate mass of this isotope, in grams, that remains t years after a 320-gram sample starts to decay. Which statement is the best interpretation of m(105) = 40 in this context?

answerOptions.content: The mass of the sample has decreased by approximately 40 grams 105 years after the sample starts to decay.
The mass of the sample has decreased by approximately 105 grams 40 years after the sample starts to decay.
Approximately 40 grams of the sample remains 105 years after the sample starts to decay.
Approximately 105 grams of the sample remains 40 years after the sample starts to decay.

rationale: Choice C is correct. m(t) represents the mass remaining t years after decay begins, so m(105) = 40 means that approximately 40 grams remain 105 years after decay begins. Choice A confuses remaining mass with mass lost (the loss would be 320 − 40 = 280 grams). Choice B reverses the variables incorrectly. Choice D is the interpretation of m(40) = 105.
